generatedSentence: In the early morning, the old inventor, Professor Aris, was laying out schematics, a rhythmic drum of his fingers echoing the quiet lab. He would often wonder where his grand ambitions truly lay, contemplating what grand mechanics might one day revolutionize the world. A lone bluejay pecked at a fallen apple outside, a common sight for the husbands and wives in the mainstream suburban community. Aris pondered the kernels of opportunism in every discovery, trying to count the myriad circumventions needed for his latest project. A recent visit to the dentist had left him a bit recoiled, feeling a sense of jeopardy about his health, but volumes of research, full of a specific keyword, were crammed onto his desk. He felt like a hunter, almost floored by the enormity of his goal. Yet, a sense of being undone began to creep in. An overpowering syndicate, rumored to be behind industrial killings, had targeted Aris, seeing him as a potential victim. Their leader, a figure as imposing as a brown bear and as relentless as a buffalo, was a lecherous man whose downfall was long overdue. His oily charm and peculiar tic were infamous. This adjunct organization, an unidentified 'heavyweight' in the corporate underworld, operated with a horrible selection of enforcers, their cruelties unleashed on anyone who stood onside with decency. They dismissed concepts like unity and fairness, much like umpires ignoring fouls. Their agents, often recognizable by their mohawks, thought themselves majesties in their own right, boiling with rage like kettles of water, thriving in a desolate landscape of cactus and engaging in vicarious acts of corporate espionage, fueled by illicit oregano-scented deals. Aris, anticipating their attack, had calculated defensive vectors using his lab's comprehensive inventory. He knew his plan could backfire spectacularly, but his usual bashful demeanor was replaced by grim resolve as he prepared to launch his countermeasure. The syndicate's enforcers, a collection of grotesque characters, including a self-proclaimed rockstar with chrome nutcrackers and a hulking figure who loved to drink darkly spiced concoctions, were devoid of any decency. Their disgusting acts of jobbery and their blasphemous invocations of 'their god' were well-known. A particularly large, masked brute threw a childish tantrum when his initial attack failed, requiring an immediate adjustment of Aris's strategy against these formidable enemies. He recognized their tactics as a true pestilence, designed to induce hunger and fear through fraud and a general fallacy of invincibility. Aris watched them approach, feeling a strange sense of moving forward despite their frumpy uniforms, as one particularly nasty goon was kicked back by a force field. Through sheer kindness and the valiant efforts of his secretly developed AI assistant, Aris managed a miraculous defense, turning their expected downfall into his triumph. The syndicate's plans spread like a wildfire across the dark web, proving to be a colossal mismatch against Aris's hidden genius. He watched a protective energy bubble shimmer, protecting his lab, which felt like a grand ballroom of innovation. In this secure realm, far from the night's nasty threats, the dingo-like agents of the syndicate were left confused. Aris found new joys in his solitude, sketching graffiti-like concepts onto his blueprints. The previously peculiar challenge now felt acceptable, his AI, no longer bashful, even deployed automated hacksaw drones to dismantle their vehicles. The remaining furries (the masked enforcers) made a desperate switch to their final crowbars, but Aris, amped with new knowhow, initiated an upward thrust of his drone swarm, sending them scattering as his AI assistant cackled with digital glee, a new dawn rising.
panelSentences: In the early morning, the old inventor, Professor Aris, was laying out schematics, a rhythmic drum of his fingers echoing the quiet lab. He would often wonder where his grand ambitions truly lay, contemplating what grand mechanics might one day revolutionize the world. A lone bluejay pecked at a fallen apple outside, a common sight for the husbands and wives in the mainstream suburban community. Aris pondered the kernels of opportunism in every discovery, trying to count the myriad circumventions needed for his latest project. A recent visit to the dentist had left him a bit recoiled, feeling a sense of jeopardy about his health, but volumes of research, full of a specific keyword, were crammed onto his desk. He felt like a hunter, almost floored by the enormity of his goal.

Yet, a sense of being undone began to creep in. An overpowering syndicate, rumored to be behind industrial killings, had targeted Aris, seeing him as a potential victim. Their leader, a figure as imposing as a brown bear and as relentless as a buffalo, was a lecherous man whose downfall was long overdue. His oily charm and peculiar tic were infamous. This adjunct organization, an unidentified 'heavyweight' in the corporate underworld, operated with a horrible selection of enforcers, their cruelties unleashed on anyone who stood onside with decency. They dismissed concepts like unity and fairness, much like umpires ignoring fouls. Their agents, often recognizable by their mohawks, thought themselves majesties in their own right, boiling with rage like kettles of water, thriving in a desolate landscape of cactus and engaging in vicarious acts of corporate espionage, fueled by illicit oregano-scented deals.

Aris, anticipating their attack, had calculated defensive vectors using his lab's comprehensive inventory. He knew his plan could backfire spectacularly, but his usual bashful demeanor was replaced by grim resolve as he prepared to launch his countermeasure. The syndicate's enforcers, a collection of grotesque characters, including a self-proclaimed rockstar with chrome nutcrackers and a hulking figure who loved to drink darkly spiced concoctions, were devoid of any decency. Their disgusting acts of jobbery and their blasphemous invocations of 'their god' were well-known. A particularly large, masked brute threw a childish tantrum when his initial attack failed, requiring an immediate adjustment of Aris's strategy against these formidable enemies. He recognized their tactics as a true pestilence, designed to induce hunger and fear through fraud and a general fallacy of invincibility. Aris watched them approach, feeling a strange sense of moving forward despite their frumpy uniforms, as one particularly nasty goon was kicked back by a force field.

Through sheer kindness and the valiant efforts of his secretly developed AI assistant, Aris managed a miraculous defense, turning their expected downfall into his triumph. The syndicate's plans spread like a wildfire across the dark web, proving to be a colossal mismatch against Aris's hidden genius. He watched a protective energy bubble shimmer, protecting his lab, which felt like a grand ballroom of innovation. In this secure realm, far from the night's nasty threats, the dingo-like agents of the syndicate were left confused. Aris found new joys in his solitude, sketching graffiti-like concepts onto his blueprints. The previously peculiar challenge now felt acceptable, his AI, no longer bashful, even deployed automated hacksaw drones to dismantle their vehicles. The remaining furries (the masked enforcers) made a desperate switch to their final crowbars, but Aris, amped with new knowhow, initiated an upward thrust of his drone swarm, sending them scattering as his AI assistant cackled with digital glee, a new dawn rising.
rawPanelSentences: In the early **morning**, the old inventor, Professor Aris, was **laying** out schematics, a rhythmic **drum** of his fingers echoing the quiet lab. He would often **wonder** **where** his grand ambitions truly lay, contemplating **what** grand **mechanics** might one day revolutionize the world. A lone **bluejay** **pecked** at a fallen **apple** outside, a common sight for the **husbands** and wives in the **mainstream** suburban community. Aris pondered the **kernels** of **opportunism** in every discovery, trying to **count** the myriad **circumventions** needed for his latest project. A recent **visit** to the **dentist** had left him a bit **recoiled**, feeling a sense of **jeopardy** about his health, but volumes of research, full of a specific **keyword**, were **crammed** onto his desk. He felt like a **hunter**, almost **floored** by the enormity of his goal.

Yet, a sense of being **undone** began to creep in. An **overpowering** syndicate, rumored to be behind industrial **killings**, had targeted Aris, seeing him as a potential **victim**. Their leader, a figure as imposing as a **brown bear** and as relentless as a **buffalo**, was a **lecherous** man whose **downfall** was long overdue. His **oily** charm and peculiar **tic** were infamous. This **adjunct** organization, an **unidentified** 'heavyweight' in the corporate underworld, operated with a **horrible selection** of enforcers, their cruelties **unleashed** on anyone who stood **onside** with decency. They dismissed concepts like **unity** and fairness, much like **umpires** ignoring fouls. Their agents, often recognizable by their **mohawks**, thought themselves **majesties** in their own right, boiling with rage like **kettles** of water, thriving in a desolate landscape of **cactus** and engaging in **vicarious** acts of corporate espionage, fueled by illicit **oregano**-scented deals.

Aris, anticipating their attack, had calculated defensive **vectors** using his lab's comprehensive **inventory**. He knew his plan could **backfire** spectacularly, but his usual **bashful** demeanor was replaced by grim resolve as he prepared to **launch** his countermeasure. The syndicate's enforcers, a collection of grotesque characters, including a self-proclaimed **rockstar** with chrome **nutcrackers** and a hulking figure who loved to **drink** **darkly** spiced concoctions, were devoid of any **decency**. Their **disgusting** acts of **jobbery** and their blasphemous invocations of 'their **god**' were well-known. A particularly large, **masked** brute threw a childish **tantrum** when his initial attack failed, requiring an immediate **adjustment** of Aris's strategy against these formidable **enemies**. He recognized their tactics as a true **pestilence**, designed to induce **hunger** and fear through **fraud** and a general **fallacy** of invincibility. Aris watched them approach, feeling a strange sense of moving **forward** despite their frumpy uniforms, as one particularly nasty goon was **kicked** back by a force field.

Through sheer **kindness** and the **valiant** efforts of his secretly developed AI assistant, Aris managed a miraculous defense, turning their expected **downfall** into his triumph. The syndicate's plans spread like a **wildfire** across the dark web, proving to be a colossal **mismatch** against Aris's hidden genius. He watched a protective energy **bubble** shimmer, protecting his lab, which felt like a grand **ballroom** of innovation. In this secure **realm**, far from the **night**'s **nasty** threats, the **dingo**-like agents of the syndicate were left confused. Aris found new **joys** in his solitude, sketching **graffiti**-like concepts onto his blueprints. The previously **peculiar** challenge now felt **acceptable**, his AI, no longer **bashful**, even deployed automated **hacksaw** drones to dismantle their vehicles. The remaining **furries** (the masked enforcers) made a desperate **switch** to their final **crowbars**, but Aris, **amped** with new **knowhow**, initiated an **upward** thrust of his drone swarm, sending them scattering as his AI assistant **cackled** with digital glee, a new dawn rising.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone, elderly inventor with a long white beard and a glowing cybernetic eyepiece. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elderly inventor at a cluttered desk, surrounded by holographic schematics, looking thoughtfully at a bluejay pecking an apple outside a window, eye-level shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A shadowy, hulking figure with a peculiar facial tic, surrounded by a group of menacing agents with mohawks and oily uniforms, viewed from a low angle, emphasizing their menacing presence. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elderly inventor, now wearing a sleek protective suit, launching a barrage of small, intricate drones from a hidden console, focused on the intense action, over-the-shoulder shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The menacing agents with crowbars being lifted into the air by an upward-thrusting swarm of glowing drones, scattering in disarray against a backdrop of a rising sun, wide shot.

definitions.definition: To place down in a position of rest, or in a horizontal position.
definitions.example: A shower of rain lays the dust.

definitions.definition: To cause to subside or abate.
To prepare (a plan, project etc.); to set out, establish (a law, principle).
To install certain building materials, laying one thing on top of another.
definitions.example: lay brick;  lay flooring

definitions.definition: To produce and deposit an egg.
definitions.example: Did dinosaurs lay their eggs in a nest?

definitions.definition: To bet (that something is or is not the case).
definitions.example: I'll lay that he doesn't turn up on Monday.

definitions.definition: To deposit (a stake) as a wager; to stake; to risk.
To have sex with.
To take a position; to come or go.
definitions.example: to lay forward;  to lay aloft

definitions.definition: To state; to allege.
definitions.example: to lay the venue

definitions.definition: To point; to aim.
definitions.example: to lay a gun

definitions.definition: (ropemaking) To put the strands of (a rope, a cable, etc.) in their proper places and twist or unite them.
definitions.example: to lay a cable or rope

definitions.definition: To place and arrange (pages) for a form upon the imposing stone.
To place (new type) properly in the cases.
To apply; to put.
To impose (a burden, punishment, command, tax, etc.).
definitions.example: to lay a tax on land

definitions.definition: To impute; to charge; to allege.
To present or offer.
definitions.example: to lay an indictment in a particular county;   to lay a scheme before one

definitions.definition: To don or put on (tefillin (phylacteries)).
A percussive musical instrument spanned with a thin covering on at least one end for striking, forming an acoustic chamber, affecting what materials are used to make it; a membranophone.
Any similar hollow, cylindrical object.
definitions.example: Replace the drum unit of your printer.

definitions.definition: A barrel or large cylindrical container for liquid transport and storage.
definitions.example: The restaurant ordered ketchup in 50-gallon drums.

definitions.definition: The encircling wall that supports a dome or cupola.
Any of the cylindrical blocks that make up the shaft of a pillar.
A drumfish (family Sciaenidae).
A tip; a piece of information.
The practice of taking advantage of any situations or people to achieve an end, often with no regard for principles or consequences.
The part of the day from dawn to noon.
definitions.example: I'll see you tomorrow morning.

definitions.definition: The part of the day between midnight and noon.
The early part of anything.
The first alcoholic drink of the day; a morning draught.
A greeting said in the morning; shortening of good morning
The branch of physics that deals with the action of forces on material objects with mass
The design and construction of machines.
(writing) Spelling and punctuation.
Operation in general; workings.
definitions.example: the mechanics of a board game

definitions.definition: The master of a house; the head of a family; a householder.
A tiller of the ground; a husbandman.
A prudent or frugal manager.
A man in a marriage or marital relationship, especially in relation to his spouse.
definitions.example: You should start dating so you can find a suitable husband.

definitions.definition: The male of a pair of animals.
A manager of property; one who has the care of another's belongings, owndom, or interests; a steward; an economist.
A large cushion with arms meant to support a person in the sitting position.
definitions.example: While reading her book, Sally leaned back against her husband, wishing it were the human kind.

definitions.definition: A polled tree; a pollard.
To manage or administer carefully and frugally; use to the best advantage; economise.
definitions.example: For my means, I'll husband them so well, / They shall go far. — Shakespeare.

definitions.definition: To conserve.
To till; cultivate; farm; nurture.
To provide with a husband.
To engage or act as a husband to; assume the care of or responsibility for; accept as one's own.
The core, center, or essence of an object or system.
definitions.example: the kernel of an argument

definitions.definition: The central (usually edible) part of a nut, especially once the hard shell has been removed.
A single seed or grain, especially of corn or wheat.
The stone of certain fruits, such as peaches or plums.
A small mass around which other matter is concreted; a nucleus; a concretion or hard lump in the flesh.
The central part of many computer operating systems which manages the system's resources and the communication between hardware and software components.
definitions.example: The Linux kernel is open-source.

definitions.definition: The core engine of any complex software system.
A function used to define an integral transform.
definitions.example: The Dirichlet kernel convolved with a function yields its Fourier series approximation.

definitions.definition: A set of pairs of a mapping's domain which are mapped to the same value.
For a given function (especially a linear map between vector spaces), the set of elements in the domain which are mapped to zero; (formally) given f : X → Y, the set {x ∈ X : f(x) = 0}.
definitions.example: If a function is continuous then its kernel is a closed set.

definitions.definition: For a category with zero morphisms: the equalizer of a given morphism and the zero morphism which is parallel to that given morphism.
(fuzzy set theory) The set of members of a fuzzy set that are fully included (i.e., whose grade of membership is 1).
The human clitoris.
The nucleus and electrons of an atom excluding its valence electrons.
An instance of someone being killed.
(usually as make a killing) A large amount of money.
definitions.example: He made a killing on the stock market.

definitions.definition: The act of counting or tallying a quantity.
definitions.example: Give the chairs a quick count to check if we have enough.

definitions.definition: The result of a tally that reveals the number of items in a set; a quantity counted.
A countdown.
A charge of misconduct brought in a legal proceeding.
The number of balls and strikes, respectively, on a batter's in-progress plate appearance.
definitions.example: He has a 3–2 count with the bases loaded.

definitions.definition: An object of interest or account; value; estimation.
To recite numbers in sequence.
definitions.example: Can you count to a hundred?

definitions.definition: To determine the number (of objects in a group).
definitions.example: There are three apples; count them.

definitions.definition: To be of significance; to matter.
definitions.example: Your views don’t count here.    It does count if you cheat with someone when you’re drunk.

definitions.definition: To be an example of something: often followed by as and an indefinite noun.
definitions.example: Apples count as a type of fruit.

definitions.definition: To consider something an example of something.
definitions.example: He counts himself a hero after saving the cat from the river.   I count you as more than a friend.

definitions.definition: To take account or note (of).
To plead orally; to argue a matter in court; to recite a count.
(grammar) Countable.
The act of evading by going around (bypassing).
The act of prevailing over another by fraud or deception
Something that causes amazement or awe; a marvel.
definitions.example: Wonders of the World seem to come in sevens.

definitions.definition: Something astonishing and seemingly inexplicable.
definitions.example: The idea was so crazy that it is a wonder that anyone went along with it.

definitions.definition: Someone very talented at something, a genius.
definitions.example: He's a wonder at cooking.

definitions.definition: The sense or emotion which can be inspired by something curious or unknown; surprise; astonishment, often with awe or reverence.
A mental pondering, a thought.
A kind of donut; a cruller.
To be affected with surprise or admiration; to be struck with astonishment; to be amazed; to marvel; often followed by at.
To ponder; to feel doubt and curiosity; to query in the mind.
definitions.example: He wondered whether penguins could fly. She had wondered this herself sometimes.

definitions.definition: The place in which something happens.
definitions.example: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.

definitions.definition: Interrogative adverb, used in either a direct or indirect question: at what place; to what place; what place.
definitions.example: He asked where I grew up.

definitions.definition: In what situation.
definitions.example: Where would we be without our parents?

definitions.definition: (relative) At which, on which.
definitions.example: That is the place where we first met.

definitions.definition: The place in which.
definitions.example: He lives within five miles of where he was born.

definitions.definition: While on the contrary; although; whereas.
definitions.example: Where Susy has trouble coloring inside the lines, Johnny has already mastered shading.

definitions.definition: At or in which place or situation.
definitions.example: He is looking for a house where he can have a complete office.

definitions.definition: To which place or situation.
definitions.example: The snowbirds travel where it is warm.

definitions.definition: Wherever.
definitions.example: Their job is to go where they are called.

definitions.definition: In a position, case, etc., in which.
definitions.example: Where no provision under this Act is applicable, the case shall be decided in accordance with the customary practices.

definitions.definition: A device designed to be placed in the mouth and blown, or driven by steam or some other mechanism, to make a whistling sound.
An act of whistling.
A shrill, high-pitched sound made by whistling.
Any high-pitched sound similar to the sound made by whistling.
definitions.example: the whistle of the wind in the trees

definitions.definition: A suit (from whistle and flute).
The mouth and throat; so called as being the organs of whistling.
To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.
definitions.example: Never whistle at a funeral.

definitions.definition: To make a similar sound by forcing air through a musical instrument or a pipe etc.
definitions.example: The stream train whistled as it passed by.

definitions.definition: To move in such a way as to create a whistling sound.
definitions.example: A bullet whistled past.

definitions.definition: To send, signal, or call by a whistle.
A species of North American jay with blue feathering from the top of its head to midway down its back, Cyanocitta cristata.
One of the blue-marked species of the Old World bird genus Coracias.
To pull back, especially in disgust, horror or astonishment.
definitions.example: He recoiled in disgust when he saw the mess.

definitions.definition: To retreat before an opponent.
To retire, withdraw.
(of a firearm) To quickly push back when fired
A medical doctor who specializes in dentistry.
Danger of loss, harm, or failure.
definitions.example: The poor condition of the vehicle put its occupants in constant jeopardy.

definitions.definition: To jeopardize; to endanger.
To subdue someone by superior force.
definitions.example: We overpowered the opposing army within a couple of hours.

definitions.definition: To excel or exceed in power; to cause to yield; to subdue.
definitions.example: Bright light overpowers the eyes.

definitions.definition: To render imperceptible by means of greater strength, intensity etc.
definitions.example: The dish was OK, but the garlic slightly overpowered the herbs.

definitions.definition: To make excessively powerful.
That overpowers; so strong as to be overwhelming.
The principal current in a flow, such as a river or flow of air
(usually with the) That which is common; the norm.
definitions.example: His ideas were well outside the mainstream, but he presented them intelligently, and we were impressed if not convinced.

definitions.definition: To popularize, to normalize, to render mainstream.
To become mainstream.
To educate (a disabled student) together with non-disabled students.
definitions.example: Mainstreaming has become more common in recent years, as studies have shown that many mainstreamed students with mild learning disabilities learn better than their non-mainstreamed counterparts.

definitions.definition: Used or accepted broadly rather than by small portions of a population or market.
definitions.example: They often carry stories you won't find in the mainstream media.

definitions.definition: A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.

Any of various tree-borne fruits or vegetables especially considered as resembling an apple; also (with qualifying words) used to form the names of other specific fruits such as custard apple, rose apple, thorn apple etc.
The fruit of the Tree of Knowledge, eaten by Adam and Eve according to post-Biblical Christian tradition; the forbidden fruit.
A tree of the genus Malus, especially one cultivated for its edible fruit; the apple tree.
The wood of the apple tree.
(in the plural) Short for apples and pears, slang for stairs.
The ball in baseball.
When smiling, the round, fleshy part of the cheeks between the eyes and the corners of the mouth.
A Native American or red-skinned person who acts and/or thinks like a white (Caucasian) person.
(ice hockey slang) An assist.
To become apple-like.
To form buds.
To strike or pierce with the beak or bill (of a bird).
definitions.example: The birds pecked at their food.

definitions.definition: To form by striking with the beak or a pointed instrument.
definitions.example: to peck a hole in a tree

definitions.definition: To strike, pick, thrust against, or dig into, with a pointed instrument, especially with repeated quick movements.
To seize and pick up with the beak, or as if with the beak; to bite; to eat; often with up.
To do something in small, intermittent pieces.
definitions.example: He has been pecking away at that project for some time now.

definitions.definition: To type by searching for each key individually.
To type in general.
To kiss briefly.
To throw.
To lurch forward; especially, of a horse, to stumble after hitting the ground with the toe instead of the flat of the foot.
One who hunts game for sport or for food; a huntsman or huntswoman.
A dog used in hunting.
A horse used in hunting, especially a thoroughbred, bred and trained for hunting.
One who hunts or seeks after anything.
definitions.example: The hunter becomes the hunted.

definitions.definition: A person who bottles up their aggression and eventually releases it explosively.
A kind of spider, the huntsman or hunting spider.
A pocket watch with a spring-hinged circular metal cover that closes over the dial and crystal, protecting them from dust and scratches.
To cover or furnish with a floor.
definitions.example: floor a house with pine boards

definitions.definition: To strike down or lay level with the floor; to knock down.
(driving) To accelerate rapidly.
definitions.example: As soon as our driver saw an insurgent in a car holding a detonation device, he floored the pedal and was 2,000 feet away when that car bomb exploded. We escaped certain death in the nick of time!

definitions.definition: To silence by a conclusive answer or retort.
definitions.example: Floored or crushed by him. — Coleridge

definitions.definition: To amaze or greatly surprise.
definitions.example: We were floored by his confession.

definitions.definition: To finish or make an end of.
definitions.example: I've floored my little-go work — ed Hughes

definitions.definition: To set a lower bound.
Not done.
Any word used as the key to a code.
Any word used in a reference work to link to other words or other information.
A reserved word used to identify a specific command, function etc.
Any word that occurs in a text more often than normal.
To tag with keywords, as for example to facilitate searching.
To press, force, or drive, particularly in filling, or in thrusting one thing into another; to stuff; to fill to superfluity.
definitions.example: to cram fruit into a basket; to cram a room with people

definitions.definition: To fill with food to satiety; to stuff.
definitions.example: The boy crammed himself with cake

definitions.definition: To put hastily through an extensive course of memorizing or study, as in preparation for an examination.
definitions.example: A pupil is crammed by his tutor.

definitions.definition: To study hard; to swot.
To eat greedily, and to satiety; to stuff oneself.
(British slang) To lie; to intentionally not tell the truth.
(British slang) To make (a person) believe false or exaggerated tales.
One that is harmed—killed, injured, subjected to oppression, deceived, or otherwise adversely affected—by someone or something, especially another person or event, force, or condition; in particular:
definitions.example: the youngest victims of the brutal war

definitions.definition: A living being which is slain and offered as a sacrifice, usually in a religious rite.
A single act of visiting.
A meeting with a doctor at their surgery or the doctor's at one's home.
To habitually go to (someone in distress, sickness etc.) to comfort them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)
To go and meet (a person) as an act of friendliness or sociability.
Of God: to appear to (someone) to comfort, bless, or chastise or punish them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)
To punish, to inflict harm upon (someone or something).
Of a sickness, misfortune etc.: to afflict (someone).
To inflict punishment, vengeance for (an offense) on or upon someone.
To go to (a shrine, temple etc.) for worship. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)
To go to (a place) for pleasure, on an errand, etc.
A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
definitions.example: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.

definitions.definition: Strength of sound; loudness.
definitions.example: Please turn down the volume on the stereo.

definitions.definition: The issues of a periodical over a period of one year.
definitions.example: I looked at this week's copy of the magazine. It was volume 23, issue 45.

definitions.definition: A bound book.
A single book of a publication issued in multi-book format, such as an encyclopedia.
definitions.example: The letter "G" was found in volume 4.

definitions.definition: A roll or scroll, which was the form of ancient books.
Quantity.
definitions.example: The volume of ticket sales decreased this week.

definitions.definition: A rounded mass or convolution.
The total supply of money in circulation or, less frequently, total amount of credit extended, within a specified national market or worldwide.
An accessible storage area with a single file system, typically resident on a single partition of a hard disk.
To be conveyed through the air, waft.
To cause to move through the air, waft.
To swell.
(Singlish) Used to contradict an underlying assumption held by the interlocutor.
Something; thing; stuff.
The identity of a thing, as an answer to a question of what.
Something that is addressed by what, as opposed to a person, addressed by who.
(usually followed by "with," but also sometimes "would" or "might," especially in finance) In some manner or degree; in part; partly. See also what with
definitions.example: The market will calculate these higher risks in their funding costs what might result in higher lending rates.

definitions.definition: Such.
definitions.example: What a beautiful day!

definitions.definition: Why.
Used to introduce each of two coordinate phrases or concepts; both…and.
(interrogative) Which thing, event, circumstance, etc.: used interrogatively in asking for the specification of an identity, quantity, quality, etc.
That which; those that; the thing that.
definitions.example: He knows what he wants.

definitions.definition: (relative) That; which; who.
Whatever.
definitions.example: I will do what I can to help you.

definitions.definition: An expression of surprise or disbelief.
What do you want? An abrupt, usually unfriendly enquiry as to what a person desires.
definitions.example: What? I'm busy.

definitions.definition: Clipping of what do you say?
What did you say? I beg your pardon?
(typically with a) An intensifier to an adjective phrase; used to begin a sentence.
definitions.example: What a nice car.

definitions.definition: A species of bears native to northern America, Europe and Asia having brownish fur. (Ursus arctos)
Any of the Old World mammals of the family Bovidae, such as the Cape buffalo, Syncerus caffer, or the water buffalo Bubalus bubalis.
A related North American animal, the American bison, Bison bison.
A buffalo robe.
The buffalo fish.
To hunt buffalo.
To outwit, confuse, deceive, or intimidate.
To pistol-whip.
Given to excessive sexual activity and debauchery.
A precipitous decline in fortune; death or rapid deterioration, as in status or wealth.
definitions.example: Many economic and political reasons led to the downfall of the Roman Empire.

definitions.definition: The cause of such a fall; a critical blow or error.
An act of falling down.
definitions.example: a downfall of rain

definitions.definition: To fall down; deteriorate; decline.
A marble with an oily lustre.
(in the plural) Oilskins. (waterproof garment)
Relating to or resembling oil.
Covered with or containing oil.
Excessively friendly or polite but insincere.
The portion of the playing area where one can legally play the ball, puck, etc.
The side of a vehicle on which the driver primarily propels it.
Not in an offside position; In the part of the playing area where one can legally play the ball, puck, etc.
definitions.example: The goal was allowed because the attacker was onside.

definitions.definition: On the side of a vehicle from which the driver, paddler, etc. propels it.
Favourably located, from the point of view of the player taking a finesse.
In agreement or support; on board.
A herb of the mint family, Origanum vulgare, having aromatic leaves.
Other herbs with a similar flavor, including other species in the genus Origanum, and Mexican oregano, Lippia graveolens
The leaves of these plants used in flavouring food.
An individual member of the Mohawk people.
(also mohawk) A hairstyle where both sides are shaved, with the hair along the crest of the head kept long, and usually styled so as to stand straight up.
A member of a gang (the Mohocks) that terrorized London in the early 18th century.
The quality of being impressive and great.
definitions.example: the majesty of the Great Pyramids

definitions.definition: A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
(by extension) Something that is done or produced habitually or characteristically.
(abbreviation) ticket
To exhibit a tic; to undergo a sudden, semi-voluntary muscle movement.
An appendage; something attached to something else in a subordinate capacity.
A person associated with another, usually in a subordinate position; a colleague.
An unmalted grain or grain product that supplements the main mash ingredient.
A quality or property of the body or mind, whether natural or acquired, such as colour in the body or judgement in the mind.
A key or scale closely related to another as principal; a relative or attendant key.
(grammar) A dispensable phrase in a clause or sentence that amplifies its meaning, such as "for a while" in "I typed for a while".
(X-bar theory) A constituent which is both the daughter and the sister of an X-bar.
Symploce.
One of a pair of morphisms which relate to each other through a pair of adjoint functors.
Connected in a subordinate function.
Added to a faculty or staff in a secondary position.
A very large, heavy, or impressive person.
definitions.example: an intellectual heavyweight

definitions.definition: The professional boxing weight class for boxers weighing more than 190 pounds; a boxer in that division.
(uncountable) A similar division and contestant in other sports.
Of the heavyweight boxing (or similar) division.
Being relatively heavy.
Being a leader in one's field.
Important or impressive.
A thing that causes horror; a terrifying thing, particularly a prospective bad consequence asserted as likely to result from an act.
A person wearing a comic or grotesque costume in a parade of horribles.
Causing horror; terrible; shocking.
Tremendously bad.

A process by which heritable traits conferring survival and reproductive advantage to individuals, or related individuals, tend to be passed on to succeeding generations and become more frequent in a population, whereas other less favourable traits tend to become eliminated; the differential survival and reproduction of phenotypes.
The process or act of selecting.
definitions.example: The large number of good candidates made selection difficult.

definitions.definition: Something selected.
definitions.example: My final selection was a 1934 Chateau Lafitte.

definitions.definition: A variety of items taken from a larger collection.
definitions.example: I've brought a selection of fine cheeses to go with your wine.

definitions.definition: A musical piece.
definitions.example: For my next selection, I'll play Happy Birthday in F-sharp minor.

definitions.definition: A set of data obtained from a database using a query.
The ability of predicates to determine the semantic content of their arguments. Wp
A list of items on which user operations will take place. Wp
A unary operation that denotes a subset of a relation.
The free selection before survey of crown land in some Australian colonies under land legislation introduced in the 1860s. Wp
The stage of a genetic algorithm in which individual genomes are chosen from a population for later breeding. Wp
Not identified; having an unknown or unnamed identity.
To free from a leash, or as from a leash.
definitions.example: He unleashed his dog in the park.

definitions.definition: To let go; to release.
definitions.example: He unleashed his fury.

definitions.definition: To precipitate; to bring about.
Not leashed; without a leash on it.
Freed from any restraint, physical or otherwise.
definitions.example: unleashed potential

definitions.definition: The official who presides over a tennis game sat on a high chair.
One of the two white-coated officials who preside over a cricket match.
One of usually 4 officials who preside over a baseball game.
definitions.example: The umpire called the pitch a strike.

definitions.definition: The official who stands behind the line on the defensive side.
definitions.example: The umpire must keep on his toes as the play often occurs around him.

definitions.definition: A match official on the ground deciding and enforcing the rules during play. As of 2007 the Australian Football League uses 3, or in the past 2 or just 1. The other officials, the goal umpires and boundary umpires, are normally not called just umpires alone.
A person who arbitrates between contending parties.
The official who presides over a curling game.
Oneness; the state or fact of being one undivided entity.
Agreement; harmony.
A single undivided thing, seen as complete in itself.

Any of the three classical rules of drama: unity of action (nothing should be admitted not directly relevant to the development of the plot), unity of place (the scenes should be set in the same place), and unity of time (all the events should be such as might happen within a single day).
The number 1 or any element of a set or field that behaves under a given operation as the number 1 behaves under multiplication.
The peculiar characteristics of an estate held by several in joint tenancy.

The form of consensus in a Quaker meeting for business which signals that a decision has been reached. In order to achieve unity, everyone who does not agree with the decision must explicitly stand aside, possibly being recorded in the minutes as doing so.
A vessel for boiling a liquid or cooking food, usually metal and equipped with a lid.
definitions.example: There's a hot kettle of soup on the stove.

definitions.definition: The quantity held by a kettle.
A vessel for boiling water for tea.
definitions.example: Stick the kettle on and we'll have a nice cup of tea.

definitions.definition: A kettle hole, sometimes any pothole.
(collective) A group of raptors riding a thermal, especially when migrating.
definitions.example: a kettle of hawks

definitions.definition: A steam locomotive
A kettledrum.
An instance of kettling; a group of protesters or rioters confined in a limited area.
A kind of fishweir resembling a wattle or fence.
Any member of the family Cactaceae, a family of flowering New World succulent plants suited to a hot, semi-desert climate.
Any succulent plant with a thick fleshy stem bearing spines but no leaves, such as euphorbs.
Non-functional, broken, exhausted, dead.
Delegated.
Experienced or gained by taking in another person’s experience, rather than through first-hand experience, such as through watching or reading.
definitions.example: People experience vicarious pleasures through watching television.

definitions.definition: On behalf of others.
definitions.example: The concept of vicarious atonement, that one person can atone for the sins of another, is found in many religions.

definitions.definition: A directed quantity, one with both magnitude and direction; the signed difference between two points.
An ordered tuple representing a directed quantity or the signed difference between two points.
Any member of a (generalized) vector space.
definitions.example: The vectors in {\mathbb Q}[X] are the single-variable polynomials with rational coefficients: one is \textstyle x^{42}+\frac1{137}x-1.

definitions.definition: A chosen course or direction for motion, as of an aircraft.
A carrier of a disease-causing agent.
(by extension) A person or entity that passes along an urban legend or other meme.
A recurring psychosocial issue that stimulates growth and development in the personality.
The way in which the eyes are drawn across the visual text. The trail that a book cover can encourage the eyes to follow from certain objects to others.

(operating systems) A memory address containing the address of a code entry point, usually one which is part of a table and often one that is dereferenced and jumped to during the execution of an interrupt.
A one-dimensional array.
A graphical representation using outlines; vector graphics.
definitions.example: a vector image

definitions.definition: A DNA molecule used to carry genetic information from one organism into another.
To set (particularly an aircraft) on a course toward a selected point.
To redirect to a vector, or code entry point.
(operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.
definitions.example: Due to an undersized inventory at the Boston outlet, customers had to travel to Providence to find the item.

definitions.definition: (operations) A detailed list of all of the items on hand.
definitions.example: The inventory included several items that one wouldn't normally think to find at a cheese shop.

definitions.definition: (operations) The process of producing or updating such a list.
definitions.example: This month's inventory took nearly three days.

definitions.definition: A space containing the items available to a character for immediate use.
definitions.example: You can't get through the underground tunnel if there are more than three items in your inventory.

definitions.definition: (operations) To take stock of the resources or items on hand; to produce an inventory.
definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: A small, controlled fire set in the path of a larger uncontrolled fire, in order to limit the spread of the large fire by removing its fuel.

An explosion produced either by a running internal combustion engine that occurs in the air intake or exhaust system rather than inside the combustion chamber or unburned fuel or hydrocarbons ignited somewhere in the exhaust system.
A premature explosion in the cylinder of a gas or oil engine during the exhaust or the compression stroke, tending to drive the piston in the wrong direction.
An explosion in the exhaust passages of an internal combustion engine.
(of a gun, cannon, Bunsen burner, etc.) To fire in the opposite direction, for example due to an obstruction in the barrel.
(of an engine) To experience a premature ignition of fuel or an ignition of exhaust gases, making a popping sound.
To fail in a manner that brings down further misfortune.
definitions.example: His attempt to make money by importing luxury cars backfired horribly when fuel prices tripled.

definitions.definition: Shy; not liking to be noticed; socially timid.
Indicating bashfulness.
definitions.example: a bashful look

definitions.definition: The movement of a vessel from land into the water; especially, the sliding on ways from the stocks on which it is built. (Compare: to splash a ship.)
The act or fact of launching (a ship/vessel, a project, a new book, etc.).
An event held to celebrate the launch of a ship/vessel, project, a new book, etc.; a launch party.
definitions.example: book launch

definitions.definition: To throw (a projectile such as a lance, dart or ball); to hurl; to propel with force.
To pierce with, or as with, a lance.
To cause (a vessel) to move or slide from the land or a larger vessel into the water; to set afloat.
definitions.example: The navy launched another ship.

definitions.definition: To cause (a rocket, balloon, etc., or the payload thereof) to begin its flight upward from the ground.
definitions.example: NASA launched several unmanned rockets before launching any of the Mercury astronauts.

definitions.definition: To send out; to start (someone) on a mission or project; to give a start to (something); to put in operation
definitions.example: Our business launched a new project.

definitions.definition: To start (a program or feature); to execute or bring into operation.
definitions.example: Double-click an icon to launch the associated application.

definitions.definition: To release; to put onto the market for sale
Of a ship, rocket, balloon, etc.: to depart on a voyage; to take off.
(often with out) To move with force and swiftness like a sliding from the stocks into the water; to plunge; to begin.
definitions.example: to launch into an argument or discussion

definitions.definition: (of a program) To start to operate.
definitions.example: After clicking the icon, the application will launch.

definitions.definition: A member of a rock band, or a solo artist in the genre, especially one with celebrity status.
(sometimes used attributively) A person who is renowned or revered in his or her field of accomplishment.
(sometimes used attributively) Any animal, plant, or other thing that is renowned amongst others of its type.
definitions.example: Owls are the rockstars of the bird world.

definitions.definition: An implement for cracking nuts.
Either of two birds of the genus Nucifraga in the crow family.
A bootleg mixed drink made from a blend of alcohols and fruit juices.
To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
definitions.example: He drank the water I gave him.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).
definitions.example: Jack drank the whole bottle by himself.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Everyone who is drinking is drinking, but not everyone who is drinking is drinking.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
With a dark appearance.
Faintly seen in the dark.
definitions.example: I could make out his image darkly.

definitions.definition: In a morbid manner; morbidly, sinisterly.
definitions.example: a darkly comic dystopian drama

definitions.definition: Mysteriously; forebodingly, ominously.
The quality of being decent; propriety.
That which is proper or becoming.
To cause an intense dislike for something.
definitions.example: It disgusts me to see her chew with her mouth open.

definitions.definition: Causing disgust; repulsive; distasteful.
The improper or corrupt act or conduct of public or official business for the sake of private gain.
A being such as a monotheistic God: a single divine creator and ruler of the universe.
The single deity of various monotheistic religions, especially the deity of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.
definitions.example: Dawn believes in God, but Willow believes in multiple gods and goddesses.

definitions.definition: The single male deity of various bitheistic or duotheistic religions.
A deity or supreme being; a supernatural, typically immortal, being with superior powers, to which personhood is attributed.
definitions.example: The most frequently used name for the Islamic god is Allah.

definitions.definition: An idol.
definitions.example: Leo Messi is my god!

definitions.definition: A person in a high position of authority, importance or influence.
A powerful ruler or tyrant.
An exceedingly handsome man.
definitions.example: Lounging on the beach were several Greek gods.

definitions.definition: The person who owns and runs a multi-user dungeon.
To idolize.
To deify.
To cover (the face or something else), in order to conceal the identity or protect against injury; to cover with a mask or visor.
To disguise; to cover; to hide.
To conceal; also, to intervene in the line of.
To cover or keep in check.
definitions.example: to mask a body of troops or a fortess by a superior force, while some hostile evolution is being carried out

definitions.definition: To take part as a masker in a masquerade
To wear a mask; to be disguised in any way
To cover or shield a part of a design or picture in order to prevent reproduction or to safeguard the surface from the colors used when working with an air brush or painting
To set or unset (certain bits, or binary digits, within a value) by means of a bitmask.
To disable (an interrupt, etc.) by setting or unsetting the associated bit.
To mash.
To mix malt with hot water to yield wort.
To be infused or steeped.
To prepare tea in a teapot; alternative to brew.
To bewilder; confuse.
Wearing a mask or masks
Characterized by masks
Concealed; hidden.
Personate
Having the anterior part of the head differing decidedly in color from the rest of the plumage; -- said of birds.
An often childish display or fit of bad temper.
definitions.example: Baby Shawn threw a tantrum when he was told the bicycle was not his.

definitions.definition: To throw a tantrum.
The action of adjusting something
The result of adjusting something; a small change; a minor correction; a modification or alteration
The settling or balancing of a financial account
definitions.example: The credit card company made an adjustment to my account to waive the late fee.

definitions.definition: The behavioural process of balancing conflicting needs, or needs against obstacles in the environment.
definitions.example: When Jim graduated, he found adjustment to the working world difficult.

definitions.definition: The assessment, by an insurance company, of a claim; the settlement of such a claim
Someone who is hostile to, feels hatred towards, opposes the interests of, or intends injury to someone else.
definitions.example: Crush the enemy!

definitions.definition: A hostile force or nation; a fighting member of such a force or nation.
definitions.example: Rally together against a common enemy.

definitions.definition: Something harmful or threatening to another
To make an enemy of.
Any epidemic disease that is highly contagious, infectious, virulent and devastating.
Anything harmful to morals or public order.
A need or compelling desire for food.
(by extension) Any strong desire.
definitions.example: I have a hunger to win.

definitions.definition: The crime of stealing or otherwise illegally obtaining money by use of deception tactics.
Any act of deception carried out for the purpose of unfair, undeserved and/or unlawful gain.
The assumption of a false identity to such deceptive end.
A person who performs any such trick.
A trap or snare.
To defraud
Of an object or substance, to be supported by a liquid of greater density than the object so as that part of the object or substance remains above the surface.
definitions.example: The boat floated on the water.

definitions.definition: To cause something to be suspended in a liquid of greater density.
definitions.example: to float a boat

definitions.definition: To be capable of floating.
definitions.example: Oil floats on vinegar.

definitions.definition: To move in a particular direction with the liquid in which one is floating
definitions.example: I’d love to just float downstream.

definitions.definition: To drift or wander aimlessly.
definitions.example: Images from my childhood floated through my mind.

definitions.definition: To drift gently through the air.
definitions.example: The balloon floated off into the distance.

definitions.definition: To move in a fluid manner.
definitions.example: The dancer floated gracefully around the stage.

definitions.definition: To circulate.
definitions.example: There's a rumour floating around the office that Jan is pregnant.

definitions.definition: (of an idea or scheme) To be viable.
definitions.example: That’s a daft idea... it’ll never float.

definitions.definition: To propose (an idea) for consideration.
definitions.example: I floated the idea of free ice-cream on Fridays, but no one was interested.

definitions.definition: To automatically adjust a parameter as related parameters change.
(of currencies) To have an exchange value determined by the markets as opposed to by rule.
definitions.example: The yen floats against the dollar.

definitions.definition: To allow (the exchange value of a currency) to be determined by the markets.
definitions.example: Increased pressure on Thailand’s currency, the baht, in 1997 led to a crisis that forced the government to float the currency.

definitions.definition: To extend a short-term loan to.
definitions.example: Could you float me $50 until payday?

definitions.definition: To issue or sell shares in a company (or units in a trust) to members of the public, followed by listing on a stock exchange.
definitions.example: 2007, Jonathan Reuvid, Floating Your Company: The Essential Guide to Going Public.

definitions.definition: To spread plaster over (a surface), using the tool called a float.
To use a float (rasp-like tool) upon.
definitions.example: It is time to float this horse's teeth.

definitions.definition: To transport by float (vehicular trailer).
To perform a float.
To cause (an element within a document) to float above or beside others.
Deceptive or false appearance; that which misleads the eye or the mind.
An argument, or apparent argument, which professes to be decisive of the matter at issue, while in reality it is not. A specious argument.

One of the eight players (comprising two props, one hooker, two locks, two flankers and one number eight, collectively known as the pack) whose primary task is to gain and maintain possession of the ball (compare back).
A player on a team in football (soccer) in the row nearest to the opposing team's goal, who are therefore principally responsible for scoring goals.
An umbrella term for a centre or winger in ice hockey.
The small forward or power forward position; two frontcourt positions that are taller than guards but shorter than centers.
The front part of a vessel.
An e-mail message that is forwarded to another recipient or recipients; an electronic chain letter.
A direct agreement between two parties to buy or sell an asset at a specific point in the future; distinguished from a futures contract in that the latter is standardized and traded on an exchange.
To advance, promote.
To send (a letter, email etc.) to a third party.
definitions.example: I'll be glad to forward your mail to you while you're gone.

definitions.definition: To assemble (a book) by sewing sections, attaching cover boards, and so on.
Toward the front or at the front.
definitions.example: The fire was confined to the forward portion of the store.

definitions.definition: Without customary restraint or modesty; bold, cheeky, pert, presumptuous or pushy.
Expected in the future.
definitions.example: The stock price is currently 12 times forward earnings.

definitions.definition: Ready; prompt; ardently inclined; in a bad sense, eager or hasty.
Advanced beyond the usual degree; advanced for the season; precocious.
definitions.example: The grass is forward, or forward for the season. We have a forward spring.

definitions.definition: Towards the front or from the front.
definitions.example: The bus driver told everyone standing up to move forward.

definitions.definition: In the usual direction of travel.
definitions.example: After spending an hour stuck in the mud, we could once again move forward.

definitions.definition: Into the future.
definitions.example: From this day forward, there will be no more brussels sprouts at the cafeteria.

definitions.definition: Dowdy, unkempt, or unfashionable.
definitions.example: She came to the door in a frumpy housedress and bedroom slippers.

definitions.definition: Bad-tempered.
To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.
definitions.example: Did you kick your brother?

definitions.definition: To make a sharp jerking movement of the leg, as to strike something.
definitions.example: He enjoyed the simple pleasure of watching the kickline kick.

definitions.definition: To direct to a particular place by a blow with the foot or leg.
definitions.example: Kick the ball into the goal.

definitions.definition: (with "off" or "out") To eject summarily.
To forcibly remove a participant from an online activity.
definitions.example: He was kicked by ChanServ for flooding.

definitions.definition: To overcome (a bothersome or difficult issue or obstacle); to free oneself of (a problem).
definitions.example: I still smoke, but they keep telling me to kick the habit.

definitions.definition: To move or push suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: He was kicked sideways by the force of the blast.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm) To recoil; to push by recoiling.
To attack (a piece) in order to force it to move.
To accelerate quickly with a few pedal strokes in an effort to break away from other riders.
definitions.example: Contador kicks again to try to rid himself of Rasmussen.

definitions.definition: To show opposition or resistance.
To work a press by impact of the foot on a treadle.
To die.
(smoking, of a pipe) Empty with nothing left to smoke but ash.
The state of being kind.
An instance of kind or charitable behaviour.
definitions.example: How can I thank you for your many kindnesses?

definitions.definition: A person who acts with valor, showing hero-like characteristics in the midst of danger.
Showing courage or determination; brave, heroic.
A game played on a rectangular court between two teams of two to six players which involves striking a ball back and forth over a net.
The inflated ball used in such a game.
A rapidly spreading fire, especially one occurring in a wildland area.
Greek fire, Byzantine fire.
A spreading disease of the skin, particularly erysipelas.
Something that acts quickly and uncontrollably.
A spherically contained volume of air or other gas, especially one made from soapy liquid.
A small spherical cavity in a solid material.
definitions.example: bubbles in window glass, or in a lens

definitions.definition: (by extension) Anything resembling a hollow sphere.
Anything lacking firmness or solidity; a cheat or fraud; an empty project.
A period of intense speculation in a market, causing prices to rise quickly to irrational levels as the metaphorical bubble expands, and then fall even more quickly as the bubble bursts.
definitions.example: dot-com bubble

definitions.definition: The emotional and/or physical atmosphere in which the subject is immersed.
An officer's station in a prison dormitory, affording views on all sides.
Someone who has been ‘bubbled’ or fooled; a dupe.
A small, hollow, floating bead or globe, formerly used for testing the strength of spirits.
The globule of air in the chamber of a spirit level.
A laugh.
definitions.example: Are you having a bubble?!

definitions.definition: A Greek.
Any of the small magnetized areas that make up bubble memory.

The point in a poker tournament when the last player without a prize loses all their chips and leaves the game, leaving only players that are going to win prizes. (e.g., if the last remaining 9 players win prizes, then the point when the 10th player leaves the tournament)
definitions.example: Many players tend to play timidly (not play many hands) around the bubble, to keep their chips and last longer in the game.

definitions.definition: A group of people who are in quarantine together.
To produce bubbles, to rise up in bubbles (such as in foods cooking or liquids boiling).
To churn or foment, as if wishing to rise to the surface.
definitions.example: Rage bubbled inside him.

definitions.definition: To rise through a medium or system, similar to the way that bubbles rise in liquid.
To cheat, delude.
To cry, weep.
To pat a baby on the back so as to cause it to belch.
To cause to feel as if bubbling or churning.
To express in a bubbly or lively manner.
To form into a protruding round shape.
To cover with bubbles.
To bubble in; to mark a response on a form by filling in a circular area (‘bubble’).
A large room used for dancing and banquets.
A type of elegant dance.
To take part in ballroom dancing.
An abstract sphere of influence, real or imagined.
The domain of a certain abstraction.
A scope of operation in networking or security.
A territory or state, as ruled by a specific power, especially by a king.
An otherworldly dimension or domain — magical, ethereal, or otherwise — usually ruled or created by a mystical character.
A taxonomic rank in the phylogeny of viruses, higher than kingdoms.
A pivot attached to the gunwale (outrigger in a sport boat) of a boat that supports and guides an oar, and provides a fulcrum for rowing; an oarlock (mostly US).
The period between sunset and sunrise, when a location faces far away from the sun, thus when the sky is dark.
definitions.example: How do you sleep at night when you attack your kids like that!?

definitions.definition: (Astronomy) The period of darkness beginning at the end of evening astronomical twilight when the sun is 18 degrees below the horizon, and ending at the beginning of morning astronomical twilight.
(Legal) Often defined in the legal system as beginning 30 minutes after sunset, and ending 30 minutes before sunrise.
An evening or night spent at a particular activity.
definitions.example: a night on the town

definitions.definition: A night (and part of the days before and after it) spent in a place away from home, e.g. a hotel.
definitions.example: I stayed my friend's house for three nights.

definitions.definition: Nightfall.
definitions.example: from noon till night

definitions.definition: Darkness (due to it being nighttime).
definitions.example: The cat disappeared into the night.

definitions.definition: A dark blue colour, midnight blue.
A night's worth of competitions, generally one game.
To spend a night (in a place), to overnight.
Ellipsis of good night
definitions.example: Night, y'all! Thanks for a great evening!

definitions.definition: Something nasty.
definitions.example: Processed foods are full of aspartame and other nasties.

definitions.definition: (preceded by "the") Sexual intercourse.
A video nasty.
Dirty, filthy.
Contemptible, unpleasant (of a person).
Objectionable, unpleasant (of a thing); repellent, offensive.
Indecent or offensive; obscene, lewd.
Spiteful, unkind.
Awkward, difficult to navigate; dangerous.
Grave or dangerous (of an accident, illness etc.).
Formidable, terrific; wicked.
Canis lupus dingo, a wild dog native to Australia.
A feeling of extreme happiness or cheerfulness, especially related to the acquisition or expectation of something good.
definitions.example: They will be a source of strength and joy in your life.

definitions.definition: Anything that causes such a feeling.
definitions.example: the joys and demands of parenthood

definitions.definition: Luck or success; a positive outcome.
The sign or exhibition of joy; gaiety; merriment; festivity.
To feel joy, to rejoice.
To enjoy.
To give joy to; to congratulate.
To gladden; to make joyful; to exhilarate.
Drawings or words drawn on a surface in a public place, usually made without authorization.
Informal inscriptions, figure drawings, etc., as opposed to official inscriptions.
To mark a surface with such images.
To match unsuitably; to fail to match
(usually with up) To amplify.
definitions.example: He asked the disk jockey to amp it up.

definitions.definition: (usually with up) To excite.
(usually with up) To intensify or increase.
definitions.example: 2014, Andy Paul, Amp Up Your Sales, AMACOM Books

definitions.definition: Worthy, decent, sure of being accepted or received with at least moderate pleasure
definitions.example: We need to find an acceptable present for Jeff.

definitions.definition: Barely worthy, less than excellent; passable.
definitions.example: The designs were acceptable, but they were nothing special either.

definitions.definition: That which is peculiar; a sole or exclusive property; a prerogative; a characteristic.
(canon law) an ecclesiastical district, parish, chapel or church outside the jurisdiction of the bishop of the diocese in which it is situated.
Out of the ordinary; odd; strange; unusual.
definitions.example: It would be rather peculiar to see a kangaroo hopping down a city street.

definitions.definition: Common or usual for a certain place or circumstance; specific or particular.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are peculiar to Australia.

definitions.definition: One's own; belonging solely or especially to an individual; not shared or possessed by others.
Particular; individual; special; appropriate.
A saw, with a blade that is put under tension, for cutting metal
To cut with a hacksaw.
definitions.example: This bolt is so rusted I'll have to hacksaw it off.

definitions.definition: An animal character with human-like characteristics; most commonly refers to such characters created by members of the furry subculture.
definitions.example: What percentage of furries are wolves?

definitions.definition: A member of the furry fandom.
definitions.example: The furry had designed an elaborate costume.

definitions.definition: Someone who roleplays or identifies with a furry character. (Compare therianthrope.)
A bundle of thin sticks, typically made of wood, sometimes bond in such a way that binding can be moved so that it varies the tightness of the binding.
A device to turn electric current on and off or direct its flow.
A change or exchange.
A movable section of railroad track which allows the train to be directed down one of two destination tracks; (set of) points.
A slender woody plant stem used as a whip; a thin, flexible rod, associated with corporal punishment in the United States.
A command line notation allowing specification of optional behavior.
definitions.example: Use the /b switch to specify black-and-white printing.

definitions.definition: A programming construct that takes different actions depending on the value of an expression.
definitions.example: 2004, "Curt", Can I use IF statements, and still use switches? (on newsgroup microsoft.public.word.mailmerge.fields)

definitions.definition: A networking device connecting multiple wires, allowing them to communicate simultaneously, when possible. Compare to the less efficient hub device that solely duplicates network packets to each wire.
A system of specialized relays, computer hardware, or other equipment which allows the interconnection of a calling party's telephone line with any called party's line.
A mechanism within DNA that activates or deactivates a gene.
(BDSM) One who is willing to take either a submissive or a dominant role in a sexual relationship.
A separate mass or tress of hair, or of some substance (such as jute) made to resemble hair, formerly worn on the head by women.
To exchange.
definitions.example: I want to switch this red dress for a green one.

definitions.definition: To change (something) to the specified state using a switch.
definitions.example: Switch the light on.

definitions.definition: To whip or hit with a switch.
To change places, tasks, etc.
definitions.example: I want to switch to a different seat.

definitions.definition: To get angry suddenly; to quickly or unreasonably become enraged.
To swing or whisk.
definitions.example: to switch a cane

definitions.definition: To be swung or whisked.
definitions.example: The angry cat's tail switched back and forth.

definitions.definition: To trim.
definitions.example: to switch a hedge

definitions.definition: To turn from one railway track to another; to transfer by a switch; generally with off, from, etc.
definitions.example: to switch off a train; to switch a car from one track to another

definitions.definition: To shift to another circuit.
Pertaining to riding with the front and back feet swapped round compared to one's normal position.
Pertaining to skiing backwards.
The upper part; the top.
Directed toward a higher place.
definitions.example: with upward eye; with upward course

definitions.definition: In a direction from lower to higher; toward a higher place; in a course toward the source or origin
definitions.example: We ran upward

definitions.definition: In the upper parts; above.
Yet more; indefinitely more; above; over.
The knowledge and skill to be able to (do something correctly).
definitions.example: Marlo has the know-how to fix your computer.

definitions.definition: An iron or steel bar, often with a flattened end which may also be hook-shaped, to be used as a lever to manually force things apart.
An electrical circuit that prevents an overvoltage from causing damage.
A type of cocktail made with only Crown Royal whiskey and lemon lime soda.
To use force to move. To prise.
To make a sharp, broken noise or cry, as a hen or goose does.
To laugh with a broken sound similar to a hen's cry.
definitions.example: The witch cackled evilly.

definitions.definition: To talk in a silly manner; to prattle.
